creator: sa2c@sa2c.net
text:
On 2006/09/02 0:45, Masahiro Teramoto wrote:
> I make a little bit change, based on NIIMI-san's port.
> Tested on 5.5-RELEASE(i386) and 6.1-RELEASE(i386), and I can't test on
> amd64.
> 
> - update to VMware Server 1.0.1 build 29996
> - revert to former fetch and extract procedures
>     because it requires agreement to EULA to download VMware Server.
>     I think that to download VMware Server archive without
>     EULA is inappropriate.

* "Whom" should be "matusita@jp.FreeBSD.org" because I just modified 
Matsushita-san's ports.

* Is there any reason to change ${PORTNAME}:${PKGNAMESUFFIX} from 
vmware-server:-{guestd,tools} to vmware:-server-{guestd,tools}?

* I don't like installing from virtual CD because the host is server 
product.  Users of guest OS do not necessarily have rights to access 
host OS or VMware Server Console.

If the original method is inappropriate, I prefer the following.
> MASTER_SITES=	http://www.vmware.com/download/server/
> do-fetch:
> 	@${ECHO} Please fetch ${DISTFILES} (TAR Binary for Linux) from 
> 	@${ECHO} ${MASTER_SITES} and place it in
> 	@${ECHO} ${DISTDIR}.

* NO_PACKAGE is not required.  Quote from Porter's Handbook:
http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/porting-restrictions.html#AEN2582
> NO_CDROM or NO_PACKAGE should not be set along with RESTRICTED
> since the latter variable implies the former ones.

* post-patch "magic" seems not to be required anymore.

Thanks,
-- 
NIIMI Satoshi
